---
title: "Untitled"
author: "Johan Zicola"
date: '2025-01-06'
output: html_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```

# Objective

Di and Kathi proceeded to the RNA extraction and mRNA-seq library preparation of 8 different insect species. The libraries were sequenced in 100+100 PE mode by BGI.


# Insect species

Insects collected and identified by Di:

| library | species                  | basionym             | order       | NCBI taxonomy ID |
|---------|--------------------------|----------------------|-------------|------------------|
| 01_Ad   | Anchomenus dorsalis      |                      | Coleoptera  | 586010           |
| 02_Pm   | Pterostichus melanarius  |                      | Coleoptera  | 60768            |
| 03_Zt   | Zabrus tenebrioides      | Carabus tenebrioides | Coleoptera  | 272042           |
| 04_Asp  | Amara similata           | Harpalus similatus   | Coleoptera  | 272013           |
| 05_G    | Harpalus affinis         |                      | Coleoptera  | 247283           |
| 06_Ol   | Harpalus eous            |                      | Coleoptera  | 2107211          |
| 07_Pr   | Poecilus cupreus         | Pterostichus cupreus | Coleoptera  | 270614           |
| 08_Ar   | Aphidius rhopalosiphi    |                      | Hymenoptera | 55891            |

+ morphology and species attributes: https://www.gbif.org/species/search?q=
+ Full taxonomy: https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/Taxonomy/Browser/wwwtax.cgi

Besides Aphidius rhopalosiphi (parasitic wasp, Hymenoptera), all other 7 species are Coleoptera.

The goal is to compare transcripts of different species and see if they are targeted by the designed dsRNAs used to kill the CSFB.

* Assemble the transcripts for each species
* Get the full list of transcripts and the list of longest ORFs


Off-target analysis done by the company:

* Map the designed dsRNAs (50 or so designed) to these transcripts with allowed mismatches (do 0, 1, and 2)
* Get a list of candidate target transcripts

Check Cedden 2024 (https://resjournals.onlinelibrary.wiley.com/doi/10.1111/imb.12942) for the dsRNA design and off-target analysis. He developped an online tool to design dsRNAs (https://dsrip.uni-goettingen.de/efficiency).

# Data

Data are in `/usr/users/npghpc/data/NGS_data/BGI_data/F22FTSEUHT1495_LIBgsmfR_judi_8_insects`

Summary statistics for raw data:

| Sample | Length(bp) | TotalReads            |
|--------|------------|-----------------------|
| 01_Ad  | 100;100    |           64,769,548  |
| 02_Pm  | 100;100    |           56,305,457  |
| 03_Zt  | 100;100    |           50,054,875  |
| 04_Asp | 100;100    |           42,581,758  |
| 05_G   | 100;100    |           41,668,796  |
| 06_Ol  | 100;100    |           57,482,693  |
| 07_Pr  | 100;100    |           47,960,411  |
| 08_Ar  | 100;100    |           44,601,099  |

We have between 44 and 64 M paired-end reads.

fastqc

```{bash, eval=FALSE}
f *fq.gz > list_fastq.txt

while read i; do srun --partition medium fastqc $i -o fastqc_reports/ & done < list_fastq.txt
```


# Pipeline

https://southgreenplatform.github.io/tutorials//bioanalysis/trinity/#trimmomatic

* Trim data with cutadapt to remove adapters

# Trimming

Note that Trinity will skip reads of less than 25 bp so I can set the minimum size to 25 nt.

Remove 3' adapters from read 1 and read 2 and trim by quality, allowing no Ns.

```{bash, eval=FALSE}

#!/bin/bash
#
#SBATCH --job-name=trimming_cutadapt
#SBATCH --nodes=1
#SBATCH --ntasks=1 
#SBATCH --mem=16000
#SBATCH --output=slurm.%A.%a.out
#SBATCH --error=slurm.%A.%a.err
#SBATCH --partition=medium
#SBATCH --time=02:00:00
#SBATCH --constraint=scratch
#SBATCH --mail-type=END
#SBATCH --mail-user=johan.zicola@uni-goettingen.de
#SBATCH --array=1-8


mem_gbytes=$(( $SLURM_MEM_PER_NODE/1024 ))

echo "Starting at $(date)"
echo "Job submitted to the ${SLURM_JOB_PARTITION} partition, the default partition on ${SLURM_CLUSTER_NAME}"
echo "Job name: ${SLURM_JOB_NAME}, Job ID: ${SLURM_JOB_ID}"
echo "I have ${SLURM_CPUS_ON_NODE} CPUs and ${mem_gbytes}GiB of RAM on compute node $(hostname)"

module load cutadapt/4.4

file_name=$(find . -type f -name "*fq.gz" | \
cut -d_ -f1-4 | sort | uniq | sed -n ${SLURM_ARRAY_TASK_ID}p)

cutadapt -a AGATCGGAAGAGCACACGTCTGAACTCCAGTCA \
    -A AGATCGGAAGAGCGTCGTGTAGGGAAAGAGTGT \
    --quality-base 33 --max-n 0 --minimum-length 25 -o ${file_name}_1.trimmed.fq \
    -p ${file_name}_2.trimmed.fq ${file_name}_1.fq.gz ${file_name}_2.fq.gz
    
```

The Ar sample shows a polyA adaptor contamination that is gradual from the beginning to the end of the read. It also contains a lower GC content than all other samples. 

Number of reads after trimming:

```{bash, eval=FALSE}
for i in *fq; do srun --partition medium fastqc $i -o fastqc_output/ & done

cut -f1,6 multiqc_general_stats.txt
```


| Sample      | TotalReads  |
|-------------|-------------|
| 01_Ad_L1_1  | 63,577,928  |
| 01_Ad_L1_2  | 63,577,928  |
| 02_Pm_L1_1  | 55,269,387  |
| 02_Pm_L1_2  | 55,269,387  |
| 03_Zt_L1_1  | 49,128,635  |
| 03_Zt_L1_2  | 49,128,635  |
| 04_Asp_L1_1 | 41,800,974  |
| 04_Asp_L1_2 | 41,800,974  |
| 05_G_L1_1   | 40,906,340  |
| 05_G_L1_2   | 40,906,340  |
| 06_Ol_L1_1  | 56,417,880  |
| 06_Ol_L1_2  | 56,417,880  |
| 07_Pr_L1_1  | 47,070,196  |
| 07_Pr_L1_2  | 47,070,196  |
| 08_Ar_L1_1  | 43,759,260  |
| 08_Ar_L1_2  | 43,759,260  |


# Trinity (v2.15.1)

Use apptainer, the easiest solution to deal with all Trinity dependencies.

```{bash, eval=FALSE}
#!/bin/bash
#SBATCH --job-name=Trinity
#SBATCH --nodes=1
#SBATCH --time=12:00:00
#SBATCH --cpus-per-task=8
#SBATCH --mem=110gb
#SBATCH --partition=medium
#SBATCH --mail-type=END
#SBATCH --mail-user=johan.zicola@uni-goettingen.de
#SBATCH --output=Trinity.%J.out
#SBATCH --error=Trinity.%J.err

mem_gbytes=$(( $SLURM_MEM_PER_NODE/1024 ))

echo "Starting at $(date)"
echo "Job submitted to the ${SLURM_JOB_PARTITION} partition, the default partition on ${SLURM_CLUSTER_NAME}"
echo "Job name: ${SLURM_JOB_NAME}, Job ID: ${SLURM_JOB_ID}"
echo "I have ${SLURM_CPUS_ON_NODE} CPUs and ${mem_gbytes}GiB of RAM on compute node $(hostname)"

module purge

module load apptainer

PATH_DIR="/usr/users/npghpc/data/NGS_data/BGI_data/F22FTSEUHT1495_LIBgsmfR_judi_8_insects"

apptainer exec --bind $PATH_DIR -e /usr/users/zicola/bin/trinityrnaseq.v2.15.1.simg  Trinity \
          --max_memory 100G \
          --CPU $SLURM_CPUS_PER_TASK \
          --seqType fq \
          --SS_lib_type RF \
          --left ${PATH_DIR}/$INPUT_FASTQ_LEFT  \
          --right ${PATH_DIR}/$INPUT_FASTQ_RIGHT \
          --output ${PATH_DIR}/$OUTPUT_DIR

```

Loop of over each sample:

```{bash, eval=FALSE}

while read i; do
  echo "sbatch  --export=INPUT_FASTQ_LEFT=trimmed_reads/${i}_L1_1.trimmed.fq,INPUT_FASTQ_RIGHT=trimmed_reads/${i}_L1_2.trimmed.fq,OUTPUT_DIR=trinity_output/${i}/${i}_trinity trinity_run.sh"
  sbatch  --export=INPUT_FASTQ_LEFT=trimmed_reads/${i}_L1_1.trimmed.fq,INPUT_FASTQ_RIGHT=trimmed_reads/${i}_L1_2.trimmed.fq,OUTPUT_DIR=trinity_output/${i}/${i}_trinity trinity_run.sh
done < list

```


```{bash, eval=FALSE}
while read i; do seqkit stats $i; done <<<$(f *trinity.Trinity.fasta )
file                                 format  type  num_seqs      sum_len  min_len  avg_len  max_len
./02_Pm/02_Pm_trinity.Trinity.fasta  FASTA   DNA    140,145  116,247,368      177    829.5   26,406
file                               format  type  num_seqs      sum_len  min_len  avg_len  max_len
./05_G/05_G_trinity.Trinity.fasta  FASTA   DNA    104,390  100,387,029      177    961.7   25,018
file                                 format  type  num_seqs     sum_len  min_len  avg_len  max_len
./03_Zt/03_Zt_trinity.Trinity.fasta  FASTA   DNA     76,934  86,902,887      182  1,129.6   26,778
file                                   format  type  num_seqs     sum_len  min_len  avg_len  max_len
./04_Asp/04_Asp_trinity.Trinity.fasta  FASTA   DNA     72,481  82,598,703      174  1,139.6   24,631
file                                 format  type  num_seqs      sum_len  min_len  avg_len  max_len
./01_Ad/01_Ad_trinity.Trinity.fasta  FASTA   DNA    103,137  110,493,798      184  1,071.3   33,453

```

## 08_Ar

Time out

```{bash, eval=FALSE}
============ Job Information ==========================
JobID: 5037516
JobName: Trinity
Submitted: 2025-01-15T18:04:05
Started: 2025-01-16T09:51:54
Ended: 2025-01-16T21:52:17
Elapsed: 721 min, Limit: 720 min, Difference: -1 min
CPUs: 8, Nodes: 1
Estimated consumption: 192.27 core-hours
=======================================================
```

Based on the forum, I can rerun Trinity command and it will start from the last completed checkpoint


```{bash, eval=FALSE}
i="08_Ar"
sbatch  --export=INPUT_FASTQ_LEFT=trimmed_reads/${i}_L1_1.trimmed.fq,INPUT_FASTQ_RIGHT=trimmed_reads/${i}_L1_2.trimmed.fq,OUTPUT_DIR=trinity_output/${i}/${i}_trinity trinity_run.sh
```


```{bash, eval=FALSE}
============ Job Information ==========================
JobID: 5056408
JobName: Trinity
Submitted: 2025-01-17T08:47:18
Started: 2025-01-17T08:47:24
Ended: 2025-01-17T11:05:41
Elapsed: 139 min, Limit: 720 min, Difference: 581 min
CPUs: 8, Nodes: 1
Estimated consumption: 37.07 core-hours
=======================================================
```

Finished in 139 min for the remaining steps.


# Summary assemblies

```{bash, eval=FALSE}

f *_trinity.Trinity.fasta -exec seqkit stats {} \; | grep -v "file"
./02_Pm/02_Pm_trinity.Trinity.fasta  FASTA   DNA    140,145  116,247,368      177    829.5   26,406
./05_G/05_G_trinity.Trinity.fasta  FASTA   DNA    104,390  100,387,029      177    961.7   25,018
./03_Zt/03_Zt_trinity.Trinity.fasta  FASTA   DNA     76,934  86,902,887      182  1,129.6   26,778
./04_Asp/04_Asp_trinity.Trinity.fasta  FASTA   DNA     72,481  82,598,703      174  1,139.6   24,631
./06_Ol/06_Ol_trinity.Trinity.fasta  FASTA   DNA    100,481  96,388,352      178    959.3   20,450
./08_Ar/08_Ar_trinity.Trinity.fasta  FASTA   DNA    249,888  141,043,361      188    564.4   35,915
./07_Pr/07_Pr_trinity.Trinity.fasta  FASTA   DNA     92,572  108,290,220      180  1,169.8   24,578
./01_Ad/01_Ad_trinity.Trinity.fasta  FASTA   DNA    103,137  110,493,798      184  1,071.3   33,453


```


# Quality check TrinityStats

```{bash, eval=FALSE}

path_output="/usr/users/npghpc/data/NGS_data/BGI_data/F22FTSEUHT1495_LIBgsmfR_judi_8_insects/trinity_output"

module load apptainer

apptainer exec --bind $path_output -e /usr/users/zicola/bin/trinityrnaseq.v2.15.1.simg /usr/local/bin/util/TrinityStats.pl 01_Ad/01_Ad_trinity.Trinity.fasta

```

# Contamination removal

## NCBI FCS GX

Get for each species the taxonomy id on https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/Taxonomy/Browser/wwwtax.cgi

+ Anchomenus dorsalis (NCBI taxonomy ID 586010)
+ Pterostichus melanarius (NCBI taxonomy ID 60768)
+ Zabrus tenebrioides (NCBI taxonomy ID 272042)
+ Amara similata (=Harpalus similatus) (NCBI taxonomy ID 272013)
+ Harpalus affinis (NCBI taxonomy ID 247283)
+ Harpalus eous (NCBI taxonomy ID 2107211)
+ Poecilus cupreus (=Pterostichus cupreus) (NCBI taxonomy ID 270614)
+ Aphidius rhopalosiphi (NCBI taxonomy ID 55891)

Then use the NCBI FCS GX tool to detect contaminations (https://usegalaxy.org/)

Rename the two output files with the name of the libaries.

```{bash, eval=FALSE}

for i in *Action_report.tabular; do
  sample=$(echo "$i" | cut -d_ -f1-2)
  echo "grep "^TRINITY_" $i | cut -f1 | sort | uniq > ${sample}_list_contamination.txt"
  grep "^TRINITY_" $i | cut -f1 | sort | uniq > ${sample}_list_contamination.txt
done

 wc -l *list_contamination.txt
  3452 01_Ad_list_contamination.txt
  2053 02_Pm_list_contamination.txt
   356 03_Zt_list_contamination.txt
    57 04_Asp_list_contamination.txt
   397 05_G_list_contamination.txt
   667 06_Ol_list_contamination.txt
   200 07_Pr_list_contamination.txt
  1133 08_Ar_list_contamination.txt
  8315 total
```


### Fungal presence

```{bash, eval=FALSE}

for i in *_Taxonomy_report.tabular; do 
  echo -e "\n======$i"
  awk '$9=="fung:fungi" {print $0}' $i | \
  cut -f6,7 | sort | uniq -c | sort | sed 's/^\s*//' | \
  awk '{print $2,$3,$1}'  | sort -k3nr | head
done

======01_Ad_Taxonomy_report.tabular
Mucor circinelloides 454
Mucor velutinosus 414
Mucor lusitanicus 410
Mucor irregularis 267
Mucor racemosus 118
Pilaira anomala 100
Mucor plumbeus 90
Thamnidium elegans 80
Mucor piriformis 69
Mucor mucedo 66

======02_Pm_Taxonomy_report.tabular
Mucor indicus 225
Mucor circinelloides 82
Gigaspora margarita 79
Mucor racemosus 68
Mucor lusitanicus 65
Mucor velutinosus 62
Choanephora cucurbitarum 56
Mucor irregularis 56
Massospora cicadina 54
Gilbertella persicaria 53

======03_Zt_Taxonomy_report.tabular
Massospora cicadina 32
Gigaspora margarita 25
Entomophthora muscae 21
Gigaspora rosea 21
Mucor irregularis 16
Diversispora epigaea 15
Rhizophagus clarus 12
Mucor lusitanicus 11
Mucor mucedo 11
Mucor racemosus 7

======04_Asp_Taxonomy_report.tabular
Massospora cicadina 32
Gigaspora margarita 31
Gigaspora rosea 28
Diversispora epigaea 17
Entomophthora muscae 15
Mucor circinelloides 7
Thamnidium elegans 7
Mucor irregularis 6
Geosiphon pyriformis 5
Mucor lusitanicus 5

======05_G_Taxonomy_report.tabular
Gigaspora margarita 45
Massospora cicadina 41
Gigaspora rosea 30
Entomophthora muscae 23
Diversispora epigaea 22
Rhizophagus clarus 12
Geosiphon pyriformis 9
Mucor irregularis 9
Rhizophagus irregularis 8
Cunninghamella bertholletiae 6

======06_Ol_Taxonomy_report.tabular
Mucor irregularis 60
Gigaspora margarita 40
Massospora cicadina 36
Gigaspora rosea 33
Entomophthora muscae 28
Mucor racemosus 28
Mucor circinelloides 25
Mucor mucedo 22
Mucor lusitanicus 21
Thamnidium elegans 20

======07_Pr_Taxonomy_report.tabular
Gigaspora margarita 41
Massospora cicadina 38
Entomophthora muscae 19
Rhizophagus clarus 18
Gigaspora rosea 17
Diversispora epigaea 15
Mucor piriformis 9
Linnemannia elongata 7
Podila humilis 6
Rhizophagus irregularis 6

======08_Ar_Taxonomy_report.tabular
Neoconidiobolus thromboides 35
Massospora cicadina 17
Gigaspora margarita 14
Gigaspora rosea 10
Entomophthora muscae 8
Cunninghamella blakesleeana 5
Rhizophagus irregularis 5
Backusella circina 4
Diversispora epigaea 4
Halteromyces radiatus 4

```



## NCBI FCS adaptor

Download Galaxy file and replace spaces by underscore and remove colons.

+ In Galaxy, select the output files Adaptor report => Build Dataset List
+ Download the list of files
+ Note which dataset number matches to which annotation

The software use this list of sequence: https://ftp.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pub/UniVec/UniVec

```{bash, eval=FALSE}

unzip '67_ Adaptor_reports.zip'

# Replace spaces by underscore
find . -depth -name '*' | while IFS= read -r f ; do mv -i "$f" "$(dirname "$f")/$(basename "$f"|tr ':' )" ; done

# Remove colons
find . -name "*:*" -exec rename ':' '' {} \;

# Remove prefix NCBI_FCS_Adaptor_on_data_

 rename 'NCBI_FCS_Adaptor_on_data_' '' *tabular


# Rename each file manually to replace Dataset number by library names


# Check number of transcri
for i in *tabular; do echo $i; grep -v -n "^#accession" $i | wc -l; done
NCBI_FCS_Adaptor_on_data_01_Ad_Adaptor_report.tabular
0
NCBI_FCS_Adaptor_on_data_02_Pm_Adaptor_report.tabular
3
NCBI_FCS_Adaptor_on_data_03_Zt_Adaptor_report.tabular
2
NCBI_FCS_Adaptor_on_data_04_Asp_Adaptor_report.tabular
0
NCBI_FCS_Adaptor_on_data_05_G_Adaptor_report.tabular
4
NCBI_FCS_Adaptor_on_data_06_Ol_Adaptor_report.tabular
1
NCBI_FCS_Adaptor_on_data_07_Pr_Adaptor_report.tabular
0
NCBI_FCS_Adaptor_on_data_08_Ar_Adaptor_report.tabular
9

# Check most abundant adapter type
cat *.tabular | cut -f5 | sort | uniq -c | sed 's/^\s*//' | awk '{print $2,$1}'  | sort -k2nr | grep -v "^name"
CONTAMINATION_SOURCE_TYPE_ADAPTOR:NGB01114.1:Illumina 12
CONTAMINATION_SOURCE_TYPE_ADAPTOR:NGB00201.1:NotI 2
CONTAMINATION_SOURCE_TYPE_ADAPTOR:NGB00749.1:Illumina 1
CONTAMINATION_SOURCE_TYPE_ADAPTOR:NGB00754.1:Illumina 1
CONTAMINATION_SOURCE_TYPE_ADAPTOR:NGB00846.1:NEBNext 1
CONTAMINATION_SOURCE_TYPE_ADAPTOR:NGB00972.1:Pacific 1
CONTAMINATION_SOURCE_TYPE_ADAPTOR:NGB01094.1:Rubicon 1

```

14 transcripts with Illumina adaptors.

Keep only transcript ID:

```{bash, eval=FALSE}
for i in *tabular; do 
  grep -v "^#" $i | cut -f1 > ${i%.*}.clean.tabular
done
```


## Remove contaminated transcripts

Combine contaminated transcripts with adaptors and other species and cleanup fasta and map files

```{bash, eval=FALSE}

while read i; do
cat fcs_adaptor_reports/${i}_Adaptor_report.clean.tabular \
  fcs_gx_reports/${i}_list_contamination.txt | sort | uniq > ${i}_contaminants.txt
done < list.txt

wc -l *_contaminants.txt
  3452 01_Ad_contaminants.txt
  2056 02_Pm_contaminants.txt
   358 03_Zt_contaminants.txt
    57 04_Asp_contaminants.txt
   401 05_G_contaminants.txt
   667 06_Ol_contaminants.txt
   200 07_Pr_contaminants.txt
  1142 08_Ar_contaminants.txt

```

```{bash, eval=FALSE}

while read i; do
  seqkit grep -w -v -f ncbi_fcs_gx_adaptor/${i}_contaminants.txt \
  trinity_output/${i}/${i}_trinity.Trinity.fasta > trinity_output/${i}/${i}_trinity.Trinity.cleaned.fasta
done < list.txt

[INFO] 3452 patterns loaded from file
[INFO] 2056 patterns loaded from file
[INFO] 358 patterns loaded from file
[INFO] 57 patterns loaded from file
[INFO] 401 patterns loaded from file
[INFO] 667 patterns loaded from file
[INFO] 200 patterns loaded from file
[INFO] 1142 patterns loaded from file

```

Also fix the gene_trans_map

```{bash, eval=FALSE}
# Important to use -w, otherwise, partial match will remove transcripts
# that are not in the contamination list!
while read i; do
  grep -w -v -f ncbi_fcs_gx_adaptor/${i}_contaminants.txt trinity_output/${i}/${i}_trinity.Trinity.fasta.gene_trans_map > trinity_output/${i}/${i}_trinity.Trinity.cleaned.fasta.gene_trans_map
done < list.txt

```

Gather final files

```{bash, eval=FALSE}

cd /usr/users/npghpc/data/NGS_data/BGI_data/F22FTSEUHT1495_LIBgsmfR_judi_8_insects/trinity_output

find . -type f -name *cleaned.fasta -exec cp {} summary/ \;

find . -type f -name *cleaned.fasta.gene_trans_map -exec cp {} summary/ \;

```

Rename with insect species

```{bash, eval=FALSE}

cat library_to_species.txt
01_Ad Anchomenus_dorsalis
02_Pm Pterostichus_melanarius
03_Zt Zabrus_tenebrioides
04_Asp  Amara_similata
05_G  Harpalus_affinis
06_Ol Harpalus_eous
07_Pr Poecilus_cupreus
08_Ar Aphidius_rhopalosiphi


while read i; do
  library=$(echo "$i" | cut -f1)
  species=$(echo "$i" | cut -f2)
  echo "rename $library $species *fasta*"
  rename $library $species *fasta*
done < library_to_species.txt


# Remove the .Trinity.cleaned.
rename '.Trinity.cleaned' '' *fasta*

seqkit stats *fasta
processed files:  8 / 8 [======================================] ETA: 0s. done
file                                   format  type  num_seqs      sum_len  min_len  avg_len  max_len
Amara_similata_trinity.fasta           FASTA   DNA     72,424   82,575,412      174  1,140.2   24,631
Anchomenus_dorsalis_trinity.fasta      FASTA   DNA     99,685  108,787,099      184  1,091.3   33,453
Aphidius_rhopalosiphi_trinity.fasta    FASTA   DNA    248,746  140,588,291      188    565.2   35,915
Harpalus_affinis_trinity.fasta         FASTA   DNA    103,989  100,209,791      177    963.7   25,018
Harpalus_eous_trinity.fasta            FASTA   DNA     99,814   96,095,807      178    962.7   20,450
Poecilus_cupreus_trinity.fasta         FASTA   DNA     92,372  108,133,517      180  1,170.6   24,578
Pterostichus_melanarius_trinity.fasta  FASTA   DNA    138,089  115,188,695      177    834.2   26,406
Zabrus_tenebrioides_trinity.fasta      FASTA   DNA     76,576   86,687,902      182  1,132.1   26,778


```

| file                                  | format | type | num_seqs | sum_len     | min_len | avg_len  | max_len |
|---------------------------------------|--------|------|----------|-------------|---------|----------|---------|
| Amara_similata_trinity.fasta          | FASTA  | DNA  | 72,424   | 82,575,412  | 174     | 1,140.20 | 24,631  |
| Anchomenus_dorsalis_trinity.fasta     | FASTA  | DNA  | 99,685   | 108,787,099 | 184     | 1,091.30 | 33,453  |
| Aphidius_rhopalosiphi_trinity.fasta   | FASTA  | DNA  | 248,746  | 140,588,291 | 188     | 565.2    | 35,915  |
| Harpalus_affinis_trinity.fasta        | FASTA  | DNA  | 103,989  | 100,209,791 | 177     | 963.7    | 25,018  |
| Harpalus_eous_trinity.fasta           | FASTA  | DNA  | 99,814   | 96,095,807  | 178     | 962.7    | 20,450  |
| Poecilus_cupreus_trinity.fasta        | FASTA  | DNA  | 92,372   | 108,133,517 | 180     | 1,170.60 | 24,578  |
| Pterostichus_melanarius_trinity.fasta | FASTA  | DNA  | 138,089  | 115,188,695 | 177     | 834.2    | 26,406  |
| Zabrus_tenebrioides_trinity.fasta     | FASTA  | DNA  | 76,576   | 86,687,902  | 182     | 1,132.10 | 26,778  |



Number of genes:

```{bash, eval=FALSE}
for i in *gene_trans_map; do echo $(echo "$i" | cut -d_ -f1,2); cut -f1 $i| sort | uniq | wc -l; done
Amara_similata
45382
Anchomenus_dorsalis
64702
Aphidius_rhopalosiphi
201906
Harpalus_affinis
62673
Harpalus_eous
63803
Poecilus_cupreus
56672
Pterostichus_melanarius
92836
Zabrus_tenebrioides
51663

```

| species                 | nb_genes |
|-------------------------|----------|
| Amara_similata          | 45,382   |
| Anchomenus_dorsalis     | 64,702   |
| Aphidius_rhopalosiphi   | 201,906  |
| Harpalus_affinis        | 62,673   |
| Harpalus_eous           | 63,803   |
| Poecilus_cupreus        | 56,672   |
| Pterostichus_melanarius | 92,836   |
| Zabrus_tenebrioides     | 51,663   |



## Summary contamination

| library | species                 | Transcripts w/ conta | Transcripts w/o conta | diff1 | Genes w/ conta | Genes w/o conta | diff2 |
|---------|-------------------------|----------------------|-----------------------|-------|----------------|-----------------|-------|
| 01_Ad   | Anchomenus_dorsalis     | 103,137              | 99,685                | 3,452 | 67,047         | 64,702          | 2,345 |
| 02_Pm   | Pterostichus_melanarius | 140,145              | 138,089               | 2,056 | 94,235         | 92,835          | 1,400 |
| 03_Zt   | Zabrus_tenebrioides     | 76,934               | 76,576                | 358   | 51,952         | 51,663          | 289   |
| 04_Asp  | Amara_similata          | 72,481               | 72,424                | 57    | 45,431         | 45,382          | 49    |
| 05_G    | Harpalus_affinis        | 104,390              | 103,989               | 401   | 63,019         | 62,673          | 346   |
| 06_Ol   | Harpalus_eous           | 100,481              | 99,814                | 667   | 64,282         | 63,803          | 479   |
| 07_Pr   | Poecilus_cupreus        | 92,572               | 92,372                | 200   | 56,798         | 56,672          | 126   |
| 08_Ar   | Aphidius_rhopalosiphi   | 249,888              | 248,746               | 1,142 | 202,721        | 201,906         | 815   |



# Transdecoder

Reuse script used for OPM analysis. Transdecoder needs a different output folder for each file, otherwise, it will override files of other instances.

```{bash, eval=FALSE}

#!/bin/bash
#SBATCH --job-name=TransDecoder
#SBATCH --nodes=1
#SBATCH --partition=medium
#SBATCH --cpus-per-task=1
#SBATCH --time=00:40:00
#SBATCH --mem=20gb
#SBATCH --mail-type=END
#SBATCH --mail-user=johan.zicola@uni-goettingen.de
#SBATCH --output=TransDecoder.%J.out
#SBATCH --error=TransDecoder.%J.err
#SBATCH --array=1-8

mem_gbytes=$(( $SLURM_MEM_PER_NODE/1024 ))

echo "Starting at $(date)"
echo "Job submitted to the ${SLURM_JOB_PARTITION} partition, the default partition on ${SLURM_CLUSTER_NAME}"
echo "Job name: ${SLURM_JOB_NAME}, Job ID: ${SLURM_JOB_ID}"
echo "I have ${SLURM_CPUS_ON_NODE} CPUs and ${mem_gbytes}GiB of RAM on compute node $(hostname)"

output_folder="/usr/users/npghpc/data/NGS_data/BGI_data/F22FTSEUHT1495_LIBgsmfR_judi_8_insects/transdecoder_output"
trinity_folder="/usr/users/npghpc/data/NGS_data/BGI_data/F22FTSEUHT1495_LIBgsmfR_judi_8_insects/trinity_output/summary"

file_name=$(sed -n ${SLURM_ARRAY_TASK_ID}p ${trinity_folder}/library_to_species.txt | cut -f2)
mkdir ${output_folder}/${file_name}

~/bin/TransDecoder-TransDecoder-v5.7.0/TransDecoder.LongOrfs \
    --gene_trans_map ${trinity_folder}/${file_name}_trinity.fasta.gene_trans_map \
    -t ${trinity_folder}/${file_name}_trinity.fasta \
    --output_dir ${output_folder}/${file_name} 
    && \
    ~/bin/TransDecoder-TransDecoder-v5.7.0/TransDecoder.Predict \
    -t ${trinity_folder}/${file_name}_trinity.fasta \
    --output_dir ${output_folder}/${file_name}

```




# Trinotate




Reuse script used for OPM analysis.


```{bash, eval=FALSE}

#!/bin/bash
#SBATCH --job-name=trinotate
#SBATCH --nodes=1
#SBATCH --partition=medium
#SBATCH --time=48:00:00
#SBATCH --cpus-per-task=8
#SBATCH --mem=40gb
#SBATCH --mail-type=END
#SBATCH --mail-user=johan.zicola@uni-goettingen.de
#SBATCH --output=trinotate.%J.out
#SBATCH --error=trinotate.%J.err


mem_gbytes=$(( $SLURM_MEM_PER_NODE/1024 ))

echo "Starting at $(date)"
echo "Job submitted to the ${SLURM_JOB_PARTITION} partition, the default partition on ${SLURM_CLUSTER_NAME}"
echo "Job name: ${SLURM_JOB_NAME}, Job ID: ${SLURM_JOB_ID}"
echo "I have ${SLURM_CPUS_ON_NODE} CPUs and ${mem_gbytes}GiB of RAM on compute node $(hostname)"

# Modules to load
module purge

module load rev/21.12 gcc/10.2.0 hmmer/3.3 ncbi-blast/2.11.0

# Test that all programs needed are installed
test_program(){
  command -v $1 >/dev/null || \
    { echo >&2 " $1 required but not installed. Aborting"; exit 1; }
}

test_program "diamond"
test_program "cmpress"
test_program "hmmpress"
test_program "tmhmm"
test_program "signalp6"
test_program "makeblastdb"

# Variables passed to sbatch
# NAME_SPECIES
# Output from Trinity $GENE_TRANS_MAP and $TRINITY_FASTA
# Output from Transdecoder $TRANSDECODER_PEP

# Variables
TRINOTATE_HOME="/usr/users/zicola/bin/Trinotate-Trinotate-v4.0.1"

#NAME_SPECIES is provided as argument to sbatch
sqlite_db="/usr/users/zicola/bin/Trinotate-Trinotate-v4.0.1/Trinotate_${NAME_SPECIES}.sqlite"


echo "############################"
echo "Trinotate create"
date
echo "############################"

$TRINOTATE_HOME/Trinotate --create \
    --db $sqlite_db \
    --trinotate_data_dir $TRINOTATE_HOME/TRINOTATE_DATA_DIR \
    --use_diamond

echo "###############################"
echo "Trinotate Init"
date
echo "###############################"


$TRINOTATE_HOME/Trinotate --db $sqlite_db --init \
           --gene_trans_map $GENE_TRANS_MAP \
           --transcript_fasta $TRINITY_FASTA \
           --transdecoder_pep $TRANSDECODER_PEP

echo "##############################"
echo  "Trinotate Run"
date
echo "##############################"
 
$TRINOTATE_HOME/Trinotate --db ${sqlite_db} --CPU ${SLURM_CPUS_ON_NODE} --run ALL \
  --trinotate_data_dir $TRINOTATE_HOME/TRINOTATE_DATA_DIR \
    --transcript_fasta $TRINITY_FASTA \
      --transdecoder_pep $TRANSDECODER_PEP \
      --use_diamond

echo "###########################"
echo "Generating report table"
date
echo "###########################"

$TRINOTATE_HOME/Trinotate --db ${sqlite_db} \
  --report --incl_pep --incl_trans > Trinotate_report.tsv

##################
## Misc value adds
##################

# Extract GO terms
${TRINOTATE_HOME}/util/extract_GO_assignments_from_Trinotate_xls.pl \
  --Trinotate_xls Trinotate_report.tsv -G -I > Trinotate_report.tsv.gene_ontology

# Generate trinotate report summary statistics
${TRINOTATE_HOME}/util/report_summary/trinotate_report_summary.pl \
  Trinotate_report.tsv Trinotate_report_stats

echo "##########################"
echo "done.  See annotation summary file:  Trinotate_report.tsv"
date
echo "##########################"



```

```{bash, eval=FALSE}


# Variables passed to sbatch
# Output from Trinity $GENE_TRANS_MAP and $TRINITY_FASTA
# Output from Transdecoder $TRANSDECODER_PEP

work_path="/usr/users/npghpc/data/NGS_data/BGI_data/F22FTSEUHT1495_LIBgsmfR_judi_8_insects"
while read i; do
  mkdir $i
  cd $i 
  sbatch --export=NAME_SPECIES=$i,GENE_TRANS_MAP=${work_path}/trinity_output/summary/${i}_trinity.fasta.gene_trans_map,TRINITY_FASTA=${work_path}/trinity_output/summary/${i}_trinity.fasta,TRANSDECODER_PEP=${work_path}/transdecoder_output/${i}_trinity.fasta.transdecoder.pep ../04_trinotate.sh
  cd ..
done < list_species.txt

```


Steps done before being killed

```{bash, eval=FALSE}

Running CMD: /usr/users/zicola/bin/Trinotate-Trinotate-v4.0.1/Trinotate --db /usr/users/zicola/bin/Trinotate-Trinotate-v4.0.1/Trinotate_Anchomenus_dorsalis.sqlite --LOAD_swissprot_blastp uniprot_sprot.diamond.blastp.outfmt6
-LOADING as per --LOAD_swissprot_blastp

CMD: /home/uni08/zicola/bin/Trinotate-Trinotate-v4.0.1/util/trinotateSeqLoader/Trinotate_BLAST_loader.pl --sqlite /usr/users/zicola/bin/Trinotate-Trinotate-v4.0.1/Trinotate_Anchomenus_dorsalis.sqlite --outfmt6 uniprot_sprot.diamond.blastp.outfmt6 --prog blastp --dbtype Swissprot
Can't locate DBI.pm in @INC (you may need to install the DBI module) (@INC contains: /home/uni08/zicola/bin/Trinotate-Trinotate-v4.0.1/util/trinotateSeqLoader/../../PerlLib /usr/users/zicola/perl5/lib/perl5/x86_64-linux-thread-multi /usr/users/zicola/perl5/lib/perl5 /usr/local/lib64/perl5 /usr/local/share/perl5 /usr/lib64/perl5/vendor_perl /usr/share/perl5/vendor_perl /usr/lib64/perl5 /usr/share/perl5) at /home/uni08/zicola/bin/Trinotate-Trinotate-v4.0.1/util/trinotateSeqLoader/Trinotate_BLAST_loader.pl line 9.
BEGIN failed--compilation aborted at /home/uni08/zicola/bin/Trinotate-Trinotate-v4.0.1/util/trinotateSeqLoader/Trinotate_BLAST_loader.pl line 9.
Error, cmd: /home/uni08/zicola/bin/Trinotate-Trinotate-v4.0.1/util/trinotateSeqLoader/Trinotate_BLAST_loader.pl --sqlite /usr/users/zicola/bin/Trinotate-Trinotate-v4.0.1/Trinotate_Anchomenus_dorsalis.sqlite --outfmt6 uniprot_sprot.diamond.blastp.outfmt6 --prog blastp --dbtype Swissprot died with ret 512 at /usr/users/zicola/bin/Trinotate-Trinotate-v4.0.1/Trinotate line 338.
Error, cmd: /usr/users/zicola/bin/Trinotate-Trinotate-v4.0.1/Trinotate --db /usr/users/zicola/bin/Trinotate-Trinotate-v4.0.1/Trinotate_Anchomenus_dorsalis.sqlite --LOAD_swissprot_blastp uniprot_sprot.diamond.blastp.outfmt6 died with ret 512 No such file or directory at /home/uni08/zicola/bin/Trinotate-Trinotate-v4.0.1/PerlLib/Pipeliner.pm line 187.
        Pipeliner::run(Pipeliner=HASH(0x56249c3268b0)) called at /usr/users/zicola/bin/Trinotate-Trinotate-v4.0.1/Trinotate line 736
        main::Trinotate_RUN_annotators("/usr/users/zicola/bin/Trinotate-Trinotate-v4.0.1/Trinotate_An"..., ARRAY(0x56249bf92f20), 8, "/usr/users/zicola/bin/Trinotate-Trinotate-v4.0.1/TRINOTATE_DA"..., "/usr/users/npghpc/data/NGS_data/BGI_data/F22FTSEUHT1495_LIBgs"..., "/usr/users/npghpc/data/NGS_data/BGI_data/F22FTSEUHT1495_LIBgs"...) called at /usr/users/zicola/bin/Trinotate-Trinotate-v4.0.1/Trinotate line 308
-REPORT being generated.
CMD: /home/uni08/zicola/bin/Trinotate-Trinotate-v4.0.1/util/Trinotate_report_writer.pl --sqlite /usr/users/zicola/bin/Trinotate-Trinotate-v4.0.1/Trinotate_Anchomenus_dorsalis.sqlite -E 1e-5 --pfam_cutoff DNC  --incl_pep  --incl_trans
Can't locate DBI.pm in @INC (you may need to install the DBI module) (@INC contains: /home/uni08/zicola/bin/Trinotate-Trinotate-v4.0.1/util/../PerlLib /usr/users/zicola/perl5/lib/perl5/x86_64-linux-thread-multi /usr/users/zicola/perl5/lib/perl5 /usr/local/lib64/perl5 /usr/local/share/perl5 /usr/lib64/perl5/vendor_perl /usr/share/perl5/vendor_perl /usr/lib64/perl5 /usr/share/perl5) at /home/uni08/zicola/bin/Trinotate-Trinotate-v4.0.1/util/../PerlLib/Sqlite_connect.pm line 15.


```



## Troubleshoot perl library installation

After the migration, I had to reinstall my perl libraries. I notice that some of the libraries I installed before cannot be reinstalled (`cpan install DBI`) due to a `cc1: fatal error: inaccessible plugin file /opt/sw/rev/21.12/haswell/gcc-4.8.5/gcc-10.2.0-potfoy/lib/gcc/x86_64-pc-linux-gnu/10.2.0/plugin/gcc-annobin.so expanded from short plugin name gcc-annobin: No such file or directory` error. I found on the web several posts about the annobin issue and one suggests installing a local Perl installation with Spack (https://github.com/openhpc/ohpc/issues/1364).

I may think this 'annobin' gcc plugin was present on the previous SCC nodes OS (Scientific Linux 7) but was probably removed in the current OS (Rocky Linux 8). It seems that the GCC plugin annobin can be installed on Rocky 8 (https://rockylinux.pkgs.org/8/rockylinux-devel-x86\_64/annobin-11.13-2.el8.x86\_64.rpm.html). Would it be possible to install it or do I have to install my own gcc?

Note that I tried with all versions of gcc available and all failed:

```
module purge
module load rev/20.12 gcc/7.5.0
cpan instal DBI
#cc1: error: unrecognized command line option '-fstack-clash-protection'
#cc1: error: unrecognized command line option '-fcf-protection'
#cc1: fatal error: inaccessible plugin file /opt/sw/rev/20.12/haswell/gcc-9.3.0/gcc-7.5.0-owlabd/lib/gcc/x86_64-pc-linux-gnu/7.5.0/plugin/gcc-annobin.so expanded from short plugin name gcc-annobin: No such file or directory

# Nope
module purge
module load rev/20.12 gcc/8.4.0
cpan instal DBI
#cc1: fatal error: inaccessible plugin file /opt/sw/rev/20.12/haswell/gcc-4.8.5/gcc-8.4.0-anizkf/lib/gcc/x86_64-pc-linux-gnu/8.4.0/plugin/gcc-annobin.so expanded from short plugin name gcc-annobin: No such file or directory

module purge
module load rev/20.12 gcc/9.3.0
cpan instal DBI
# cc1: fatal error: inaccessible plugin file /opt/sw/rev/20.12/haswell/gcc-8.4.0/gcc-9.3.0-a466o5/lib/gcc/x86_64-pc-linux-gnu/9.3.0/plugin/gcc-annobin.so expanded from short plugin name gcc-annobin: No such file or directory

module purge
module load rev/20.12  gcc/9.5.0
cpan instal DBI
# cc1: fatal error: inaccessible plugin file /opt/sw/rev/23.12/linux-scientific7-haswell/gcc-4.8.5/gcc-9.5.0-y5n2mx/lib/gcc/x86_64-pc-linux-gnu/9.5.0/plugin/gcc-annobin.so expanded from short plugin name gcc-annobin: No such file or directory


module purge
module load rev/20.12 gcc/10.2.0
cpan instal DBI
# cc1: fatal error: inaccessible plugin file /opt/sw/rev/20.12/haswell/gcc-8.4.0/gcc-10.2.0-tc66mg/lib/gcc/x86_64-pc-linux-gnu/10.2.0/plugin/gcc-annobin.so expanded from short plugin name gcc-annobin: No such file or directory

module purge
module load gcc/11.4.0
cpan instal DBI
#cc1: fatal error: inaccessible plugin file /opt/sw/rev/23.12/linux-scientific7-haswell/gcc-4.8.5/gcc-11.4.0-jgoe5h/lib/gcc/x86_64-pc-linux-gnu/11.4.0/plugin/gcc-annobin.so expanded from short plugin name gcc-annobin: No such file or directory
```

Red Hat documentation on Annobin: https://docs.redhat.com/en/documentation/red\_hat\_developer\_toolset/9/html/user\_guide/chap-annobin#chap-Annobin


I tried `spack install perl` but the installation failed

```
ImportError: cannot bootstrap the "clingo" Python module from spec "clingo-bootstrap@spack+python %gcc target=x86_64" due to the following failures:
    'spack-install' raised InstallError: Terminating after first install failure: ProcessError: Command exited with status 1:
    '/usr/users/zicola/.spack/bootstrap/store/linux-rocky8-x86_64/gcc-10.2.0/perl-5.34.0-hhpsqrp435rgen7twicgyvpz5tpy5tbs/bin/perl' 'Makefile.PL'
    Please run `spack -d spec zlib` for more verbose error messages
```

I tried several times `spack -d spec zlib` but it does not work.



Mnay posts about the issue with annobin. One recommendation is to install perl in local using spack.


Download archive https://metacpan.org/pod/DBI and install manually DBI-1.647.tgz


```{bash, eval=FALSE}
cd ~/bin
tar -zxvf DBI-1.647.tgz
cd DBI-1.647

perl Makefile.PL PREFIX=/usr/users/zicola/perl5/lib/perl5
make

#cc1: fatal error: inaccessible plugin file /opt/sw/rev/23.12/linux-scientific7-haswell/gcc-4.8.5/gcc-11.4.0-jgoe5h/lib/gcc/x86_64-pc-linux-gnu/11.4.0/plugin/gcc-annobin.so expanded from short plugin name gcc-annobin: No such file or directory

#make test
#make install

```


```{bash, eval=FALSE}

spack install perl

# Error message, recommends to type 'spack -d spec zlib'
spack -d spec zlib


```





# Trinotate apptainer

Download last singularity immage on https://data.broadinstitute.org/Trinity/TRINOTATE_SINGULARITY/


See the trick https://github.com/Trinotate/Trinotate/wiki/TrinotateViaSingularity

Run TmHMM and SignalPv6 separately: https://github.com/Trinotate/Trinotate/wiki/Running_Loading_Separately

> Certain software tools such as those provided by DTU Health Tech have restricted licensing that prevent distribution, and so these cannot be included in our Docker or Singularity images and must be obtained/installed separately by users. These, in particular, include both TmHMM and SignalP utilities. While these can be installed natively and automatically run by Trinotate, they cannot be automatically run via the Docker/Singularity images. Instructions below provide for execution and loading of the results into the Trinotate sqlite database.


Version of signalp6 and tmhmm used:

```{bash, eval=FALSE}

signalp6 --version
SignalP 6.0 Signal peptide prediction tool 6.0h

which tmhmm
~/bin/tmhmm-2.0c/bin/tmhmm

```


Way to call Trinotate

```{bash, eval=FALSE}

module load apptainer

PATH_DIR="/usr/users/npghpc/data/NGS_data/BGI_data/F22FTSEUHT1495_LIBgsmfR_judi_8_insects"

export TRINOTATE_HOME=/usr/local/src/Trinotate

apptainer exec --bind $PATH_DIR -e ~/bin/trinotate.v4.0.2.simg $TRINOTATE_HOME/Trinotate 

```


## Trinotate

```{bash, eval=FALSE}
#!/bin/bash
#SBATCH --job-name=trinotate
#SBATCH --nodes=1
#SBATCH --partition=medium
#SBATCH --time=48:00:00
#SBATCH --cpus-per-task=8
#SBATCH --mem=40gb
#SBATCH --mail-type=END
#SBATCH --mail-user=johan.zicola@uni-goettingen.de
#SBATCH --output=trinotate.%J.out
#SBATCH --error=trinotate.%J.err


mem_gbytes=$(( $SLURM_MEM_PER_NODE/1024 ))

echo "Starting at $(date)"
echo "Job submitted to the ${SLURM_JOB_PARTITION} partition, the default partition on ${SLURM_CLUSTER_NAME}"
echo "Job name: ${SLURM_JOB_NAME}, Job ID: ${SLURM_JOB_ID}"
echo "I have ${SLURM_CPUS_ON_NODE} CPUs and ${mem_gbytes}GiB of RAM on compute node $(hostname)"

# Modules to load
module purge
module load apptainer

# Test that all programs needed are installed
test_program(){
  command -v $1 >/dev/null || \
    { echo >&2 " $1 required but not installed. Aborting"; exit 1; }
}

test_program "tmhmm"
test_program "signalp6"

# Variables passed to sbatch
# NAME_SPECIES
# Output from Trinity $GENE_TRANS_MAP and $TRINITY_FASTA
# Output from Transdecoder $TRANSDECODER_PEP

PATH_DIR="/usr/users/npghpc/data/NGS_data/BGI_data/F22FTSEUHT1495_LIBgsmfR_judi_8_insects"

# Variables (I moved the folder TRINOTATE_DATA_DIR locally for the run to avoid path mess with apptainer)
# Was originally in /usr/users/zicola/bin/Trinotate-Trinotate-v4.0.1
TRINOTATE_DATA_DIR="${PATH_DIR}/trinotate_output/TRINOTATE_DATA_DIR"

export TRINOTATE_HOME=/usr/local/src/Trinotate

#NAME_SPECIES is provided as argument to sbatch
sqlite_db="Trinotate_${NAME_SPECIES}.sqlite"


echo "############################"
echo "Trinotate create"
date
echo "############################"

apptainer exec --bind $PATH_DIR -e ~/bin/trinotate.v4.0.2.simg $TRINOTATE_HOME/Trinotate --create \
    --db $sqlite_db \
    --trinotate_data_dir $TRINOTATE_DATA_DIR \

echo "###############################"
echo "Trinotate Init"
date
echo "###############################"

apptainer exec --bind $PATH_DIR -e ~/bin/trinotate.v4.0.2.simg $TRINOTATE_HOME/Trinotate \
   --db $sqlite_db --init \
   --gene_trans_map $GENE_TRANS_MAP \
   --transcript_fasta $TRINITY_FASTA \
   --transdecoder_pep $TRANSDECODER_PEP

echo "##############################"
echo  "Trinotate Run"
date
echo "##############################"

apptainer exec --bind $PATH_DIR -e ~/bin/trinotate.v4.0.2.simg $TRINOTATE_HOME/Trinotate \
   --CPU ${SLURM_CPUS_ON_NODE} \
   --run ALL \
   --trinotate_data_dir $TRINOTATE_DATA_DIR \
   --transcript_fasta $TRINITY_FASTA \
   --transdecoder_pep $TRANSDECODER_PEP \
   --use_diamond

echo "##############################"
echo  "Trinotate Run signalp"
date
echo "##############################"

signalp6 --fastafile $TRANSDECODER_PEP \
   --output_dir sigP6outdir \
   --format none --organism euk --mode fast


apptainer exec --bind $PATH_DIR -e ~/bin/trinotate.v4.0.2.simg $TRINOTATE_HOME/Trinotate \
   --db $sqlite_db \
   --LOAD_signalp sigP6outdir/output.gff3


echo "##############################"
echo  "Trinotate Run TMHMM"
date
echo "##############################"

tmhmm --short $TRANSDECODER_PEP > tmhmm.v2.out

apptainer exec --bind $PATH_DIR -e ~/bin/trinotate.v4.0.2.simg $TRINOTATE_HOME/Trinotate \
   --db $sqlite_db \
   --LOAD_tmhmmv2 tmhmm.v2.out

echo "###########################"
echo "Generating report table"
date
echo "###########################"

apptainer exec --bind $PATH_DIR -e ~/bin/trinotate.v4.0.2.simg $TRINOTATE_HOME/Trinotate --db ${sqlite_db} \
  --report --incl_pep --incl_trans > Trinotate_report.tsv

##################
## Misc value adds
date
##################

# Extract GO terms

apptainer exec --bind $PATH_DIR -e ~/bin/trinotate.v4.0.2.simg $TRINOTATE_HOME/util/extract_GO_assignments_from_Trinotate_xls.pl \
  --Trinotate_xls Trinotate_report.tsv -G -I > Trinotate_report.tsv.gene_ontology

# Generate trinotate report summary statistics
apptainer exec --bind $PATH_DIR -e ~/bin/trinotate.v4.0.2.simg $TRINOTATE_HOME/util/report_summary/trinotate_report_summary.pl \
  Trinotate_report.tsv Trinotate_report_stats

echo "##########################"
echo "done.  See annotation summary file:  Trinotate_report.tsv"
date
echo "##########################"



```

```{bash, eval=FALSE}

# Variables passed to sbatch
# Output from Trinity $GENE_TRANS_MAP and $TRINITY_FASTA
# Output from Transdecoder $TRANSDECODER_PEP

work_path="/usr/users/npghpc/data/NGS_data/BGI_data/F22FTSEUHT1495_LIBgsmfR_judi_8_insects"

while read i; do
  mkdir $i
  cd $i 
  sbatch --export=NAME_SPECIES=$i,GENE_TRANS_MAP=${work_path}/trinity_output/summary/${i}_trinity.fasta.gene_trans_map,TRINITY_FASTA=${work_path}/trinity_output/summary/${i}_trinity.fasta,TRANSDECODER_PEP=${work_path}/transdecoder_output/${i}_trinity.fasta.transdecoder.pep ../04_trinotate.sh
  cd ..
done < list_species.txt

```




# #####


```



```{bash, eval=FALSE}

# Test
srun --pty -p medium --mem=10G --time=01:00:00 bash

time tmhmm --short transdecoder_output/Anchomenus_dorsalis_trinity.fasta.transdecoder.pep  > trinotate_output/Anchomenus_dorsalis/tmhmm.v2.out

 Trinotate --db sqlite.db --LOAD_tmhmmv2 tmhmm.v2.out



work_path="/usr/users/npghpc/data/NGS_data/BGI_data/F22FTSEUHT1495_LIBgsmfR_judi_8_insects"
while read i; do
  mkdir $i
  cd $i 
  sbatch --export=NAME_SPECIES=$i,GENE_TRANS_MAP=${work_path}/trinity_output/summary/${i}_trinity.fasta.gene_trans_map,TRINITY_FASTA=${work_path}/trinity_output/summary/${i}_trinity.fasta,TRANSDECODER_PEP=${work_path}/transdecoder_output/${i}_trinity.fasta.transdecoder.pep ../04_trinotate.sh
  cd ..
done < list_species.txt

```



# Off-target analysis

This job will be done by the partner company Eupheria Biotech GmbH (https://www.fisaonline.de/einrichtungen-finden/details/?tx_fisaresearch_institutions%5Baction%5D=institutionDetails&tx_fisaresearch_institutions%5Bcontroller%5D=Institutions&tx_fisaresearch_institutions%5Bi_id%5D=5804&cHash=f826a8e38b4decbcd499614c8e5f051e#more)



Use Doga's script (https://figshare.com/articles/dataset/_i_in_silico_i_off-target_analysis_of_lethal_genes_in_cabbage_stem_flea_beetle/25283683?file=44671225) (scripts/off_target_prediction_lethal.py)

# COI1 validation


Di mentioned that to verify if we have the correct species, one needs to look at the cytochrome oxidase subunit 1 (COI) sequence.

01_Ad
Anchomenus dorsalis (NCBI taxonomy ID 586010)
https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/nuccore/HQ953549.1

https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/Taxonomy/Browser/wwwtax.cgi?id=586010

02_Pt
Pterostichus melanarius (NCBI taxonomy ID 60768)
https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/nuccore/MH020387.1

03_Zt
Zabrus tenebrioides (NCBI taxonomy ID 272042)
https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/nuccore/ON623806.2

04_Asp
Amara similata (=Harpalus similatus) (NCBI taxonomy ID 272013)
https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/nuccore/MH020396.1

05_G
Harpalus affinis (NCBI taxonomy ID 247283)
https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/nuccore/OK638137.1

06_Ol
Harpalus eous (NCBI taxonomy ID 2107211)
https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/nuccore/LC764887.1

07_Pr
Poecilus cupreus (=Pterostichus cupreus) (NCBI taxonomy ID 270614)
https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/nuccore/MN454673.1

08_Ar
Aphidius rhopalosiphi (NCBI taxonomy ID 55891)
https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/nuccore/JN164779.1
